page: Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epdsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores using the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 ARM architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of 3780 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.

Benchmark results show a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. While single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the average tier, multi-core performance is highly competitive. The instance achieves top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation with Clang. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with both small-block and large-block read and write operations ranking in the top tier. Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput also place in the top tier, while Redis SET operations demonstrate strong performance.

The ARM64 architecture lacks x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, AMX, and FMA, which limits its suitability for workloads relying heavily on these vector extensions. However, the high memory-to-core ratio and substantial local storage capacity offer high resource density for memory-bound and storage-heavy workloads. This hardware profile is highly efficient for multi-threaded tasks, large-scale database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. It is also capable of handling medium-to-large LLM inference text generation at a strong performance tier relative to its class.
description: Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epdsv6 family, powered by a 64-core Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz on the Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio, and includes 3780 GB of local storage. While single-core benchmarks for this instance fall in the average range, its multi-core capabilities are in the top tier, as evidenced by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Clang compilation tests. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high across both read and write operations. Lacking x86 vector extensions like AVX-512, the server is instead optimized for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 64 Neoverse-N2 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is an ARM64 server with 64 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for database and multi-core workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 server combining high-density memory, substantial local storage, and top-tier multi-core performance for demanding database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-N2 physical cores operating at 3.4 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Lacks x86 vector extensions like AVX-512 and AMX
Suited for large databases, multi-threaded compilation, and web serving
